NCT06088043.title: A Study About How Well TAK-279 Works and Its Safety in Participants With Moderate-to-severe Plaque Psoriasis During 52 Weeks of Treatment

eligibility.text: . Plaque psoriasis for at least 6 months.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient duration of disease to detect treatment effects.

eligibility.text: . Moderate to severe disease.
eligibility.justification: Moderate to severe disease severity is likely to be associated with greater treatment response and thus, a stronger signal for efficacy.

eligibility.text: . Candidate for phototherapy or systemic therapy.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient's eligibility for specific treatments, which may be required for treatment success.

eligibility.text: . Other forms of psoriasis.
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with other types of psoriasis, potentially protecting them from harm or ensuring data quality.

eligibility.text: . History of recent infection.
eligibility.justification: This criterion may protect patients from potential complications related to infections and ensure data quality.

eligibility.text: . Prior exposure to TAK-279 or active comparator.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that the study population is not confounded by prior treatment with TAK-279 or an active comparator, potentially impacting data quality.

eligibility.text: Other protocol defined inclusion/exclusion criteria apply.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that the study population is not confounded by prior treatment with TAK-279 or an active comparator, potentially impacting data quality.

NCT04167462.title: An Investigational Study to Evaluate Experimental Medication BMS-986165 Compared to Placebo in Participants With Plaque Psoriasis (POETYK-PSO-3) in Mainland China, Taiwan, and South Korea

eligibility.text: Plaque psoriasis for at least 6 months
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient sample size to detect treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Moderate to severe disease
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure the study population is representative of patients with moderate to severe psoriasis, maximizing the likelihood of detecting a treatment effect.

eligibility.text: Candidate for phototherapy or systemic therapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are eligible for potential treatments and allows for comparison with placebo.

eligibility.text: Other forms of psoriasis
eligibility.justification: This criterion may be a feasibility factor, as it could limit the study population to those with specific types of psoriasis.

eligibility.text: History of recent infection
eligibility.justification: This criterion is likely included for safety reasons, as infections can be a risk factor for certain treatments.

eligibility.text: Prior exposure to BMS-986165
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are not already receiving treatment with BMS-986165, potentially leading to adverse effects.

eligibility.text: Other inclusion/exclusion criteria may apply.
eligibility.justification: This criterion is a general safety measure and could be used for feasibility reasons as well.

endpoints.text: The Percentage of Participants With sPGA Response of 0 or 1
The Percentage of Participants With PASI 75 Response
The Percentage of Participants With PASI 90 Response
The Percentage of Participants With PASI 100 Response
The Percentage of Participants With sPGA 0 Response

The Percentage of Participants With PGA-F Score of 0 or 1
The Percentage of Participants With PSSD Symptom Score of 0
The Percentage of Participants With Pp-PGA Score of 0 or 1
The Percentage of Participants With PASI 75 Response at Week 52
The Percentage of Participants With sPGA Response of 0 or 1 at Week 52

NCT06220604.title: A Study of JNJ-77242113 for the Treatment of Participants With Moderate to Severe Plaque Psoriasis (ICONIC-ADVANCE 2)

eligibility.text: Diagnosis of plaque psoriasis, with or without psoriatic arthritis (PsA), for at least 26 weeks prior to the first administration of study intervention
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient duration of disease for detecting treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Total body surface area (BSA) greater than or equal to (>=)10 percent (%) at screening and baseline
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a minimum BSA threshold for study inclusion, impacting feasibility of data collection.

eligibility.text: Total psoriasis area and severity index (PASI) >=12 at screening and baseline
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient level of disease severity for detecting treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Total investigator global assessment (IGA) >=3 at screening and baseline
eligibility.justification: This criterion assesses the severity of psoriasis, potentially indicating a higher risk of complications or adverse events.

eligibility.text: Candidate for phototherapy or systemic treatment for plaque psoriasis
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are eligible for potential treatments, impacting feasibility of data collection and study design.

eligibility.text: Nonplaque form of psoriasis (for example, erythrodermic, guttate, or pustular)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a specific type of psoriasis is included for treatment effect detection.

eligibility.text: Current drug-induced psoriasis (for example, a new onset of psoriasis or an exacerbation of psoriasis from beta blockers, calcium channel blockers, or lithium)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are not experiencing psoriasis due to medication, potentially impacting safety and treatment efficacy.

eligibility.text: A current diagnosis or signs or symptoms of severe, progressive, or uncontrolled renal, liver, cardiac, vascular, pulmonary, gastrointestinal, endocrine, neurologic, hematologic, rheumatologic, psychiatric, or metabolic disturbances
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are healthy enough to participate in the study and avoid potential complications.

eligibility.text: Known allergies, hypersensitivity, or intolerance to JNJ-77242113, deucravacitinib or to any of the excipients or components of the study intervention
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are not allergic to the study medication.

eligibility.text: Major surgical procedure, (for example, requiring general anesthesia) within 8 weeks before screening, or will not have fully recovered from surgical procedure, or has a surgical procedure planned during the time the participant is expected to participate in the study
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are physically capable of participating in the study.

endpoints.text: JNJ-77242113 and Placebo Group: Percentage of Participants Achieving an Investigator's Global Assessment (IGA) Score of 0 or 1 and Greater Than or Equal to (>=) 2 Grade Improvement From Baseline at Week 16
